summary_strategy: Position Conner as a security engineer who builds and operates insider threat systems that are reliable, maintainable, and grounded in real-world operational constraints — emphasizing deployable controls, cross-source correlation, and responsible AI integration without overclaiming leadership or speculative impact

skills_tailoring.Languages: Reorder to prioritize Python, PowerShell, Bash, and Rust first (most relevant for automation and endpoint tooling), followed by Java, C/C++, TypeScript, SQL, Swift, Go — keep all 11
skills_tailoring.Security Tooling: Reorder to highlight CrowdStrike RTR, EDR/XDR/SIEM, vulnerability management, endpoint management, and SOAR first; add 'CrowdStrike Falcon', 'Defender XDR' from inventory
skills_tailoring.AI/ML and Research: Reorder to emphasize RAG pipelines, LangChain, vector databases, NLP for security context, retrieval tuning, and AI governance/auditability; drop 'LLM developer tooling' if space constrained but retain all core items
skills_tailoring.Frameworks and Infrastructure: Reorder to prioritize Flask, FastAPI, Docker, and MCP/FastMCP first (security tooling relevance), then React.js, Node.js, Django, Spring Boot, Next.js
skills_tailoring.DevOps and CI/CD: Reorder to highlight AWS/GCP/Azure, IAM, secrets management, container orchestration (ECS), CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure as code; add 'GitHub Actions', 'Terraform', 'Ansible' from inventory
skills_tailoring.Databases: Reorder to prioritize PostgreSQL, vector databases, Snowflake, ClickHouse, DynamoDB, Redis — keep all 10

experience_focus.must_highlight: endpoint hardening at scale, API-driven remediation automation, multi-source security data correlation, secure cloud-native deployments with IAM/secrets management
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 7,000+ endpoints, 2,900-user identity portal, 10,000+ devices, 200 hours annually saved, 500 hours annual workload reduction, 25 percent compliance improvement, 30 percent reduction in user-reported issues
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: no 'led', 'spearheaded', or leadership verbs unless directly supported; avoid 'revolutionized', 'transformed', or vague impact claims like 'significantly improved'; do not invent metrics beyond those in source

risk_controls: Never add new projects, tools, or responsibilities not explicitly present in baseline resume or analysis mapping
Do not invent metrics — only retain numeric claims directly cited in source experience bullets
Avoid corporate buzzwords like 'synergy', 'holistic', or 'end-to-end'; prefer concrete verbs: built, engineered, designed, implemented, automated
Do not rename or merge skill categories — strictly use the six baseline section names
